title: lepisorus excavatus
intro: Lepisorus excavatus, also known as the Excavated Cliff Brake, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is a type of vascular plant that is native to Southeast Asia and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Lepisorus excavatus has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and its health benefits also include anti-aging and immune-boosting properties. The plant can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in herbal remedies in the form of capsules, tablets, and topical creams.
